title: Nail psoriasis review

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documenting psoriasis type, duration, extent, distribution, nails, high-impact sites, symptoms, systemic upset, daily-life impact, and wellbeing without inferring an individual diagnosis or treatment.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact psoriasis assessment section explicitly includes nail involvement and supports documenting duration, interval change, symptoms, functional or quality-of-life impact, distribution and clinician-observed nail or skin findings without inferring diagnosis or treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documenting psoriasis type, duration, extent, distribution, nails, high-impact sites, symptoms, systemic upset, daily-life impact, and wellbeing without inferring an individual diagnosis or treatment.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1.1–1.2.1.12 — assessment, severity, sites, and impact

referral.text: Supports asking about joint symptoms and recording clinician-led specialist assessment or referral only when completed or arranged.
referral.rationale: Joint-symptom and clinician-led referral qualifiers were reviewed, but no arthritis diagnosis or referral is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports asking about joint symptoms and recording clinician-led specialist assessment or referral only when completed or arranged.

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2.1–1.2.2.4 — psoriatic arthritis assessment and referral qualifiers

additions.text: Supports documenting psoriasis type, duration, extent, distribution, nails, high-impact sites, symptoms, systemic upset, daily-life impact, and wellbeing without inferring an individual diagnosis or treatment.

source_limitations: Specific nail signs and count, skin psoriasis extent, joint symptoms, fungal and trauma differential, diagnosis, severity score, investigations, treatment, monitoring, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
